title: Lab 44: Filesystem By Label
description: Create an ext4 filesystem by label and mount it persistently.

tasks: On /dev/sdb, create a GPT partition of 600 MiB for an ext4 filesystem.
Format the new partition with the filesystem label DATA44 and mount it at /data44.
Configure the mount persistently in /etc/fstab by using LABEL=DATA44.
hints: The title already tells you the required persistence method.
Use LABEL=DATA44, not a device path or UUID.
checks: blkid -o value -s LABEL /dev/sdb1 | grep -qx DATA44 && findmnt -n /data44 | grep -Fq '/data44'
solution_outline: Partition /dev/sdb with a 600 MiB GPT partition.
Create an ext4 filesystem labeled DATA44 and mount it.
Persist the mount in /etc/fstab using LABEL=DATA44.
task_titles: Format and mount the filesystem by label
Persist the LABEL mount in fstab
